issues_found: No ImageNet experiments despite 7+ iterations of flagging. Project constraints mandate ImageNet. Paper claims 'sufficiently overparameterized problems' but evidence is CIFAR-10/100 only (32x32, <=15M params). This is the single largest barrier to top-venue acceptance. Zero diagnostic work has been done (no smoke test, no data path verification).

Statistical power insufficient for null-result claims. N=3 gives ~15-20% power for 0.5% effects. TOST equivalence confirmed in only 6/12 comparisons at delta=1.0%. A null-result paper requires higher evidentiary burden than a positive-result paper.

CSI listed as a primary contribution (Section 1.3, item 2) despite zero predictive value (rho<0.3, p>0.3) and arbitrary component weights (0.4, 0.3, 0.3). Sensitivity analysis tests only 3 nearly-identical weight sets. Flagged for 6+ iterations without demotion. This is a <1 hour text fix that has been deferred every iteration.
Paper references Appendix A but no appendix exists. 6th consecutive iteration of this issue. Missing proof sketches for theorems referenced in framework section.

Abstract implies full factorial design ('7 methods, 2 optimizers, 2 architectures, 2 datasets') suggesting 168 runs. Actual: 84 (ResNet-20) + 21 (VGG-16-BN SGD/CIFAR-10 only) = 105. Reviewers expecting VGG+AdamW or VGG+CIFAR-100 will be disappointed.

NoBN ablation data from iter_005 exists (constant: 87.74, CWD: 87.62) but only 2/7 methods tested. Section 6.2 presents these as evidence for the BN mechanism hypothesis, but spread 0.11pp WITHOUT BN is narrower than 0.25pp WITH BN, contradicting the hypothesis. Presenting 2/7 as evidence for a 7-method claim is misleading.

Section 5.7 (Cumulative Alignment Analysis) presents two non-significant correlations (rho=-0.161, p=0.485; rho=0.107, p=0.645) and occupies substantial space for a double-null finding. While now correctly framed as null results (improvement from iter_010), the section adds limited insight beyond AIS invariance already covered.
Orphan figure certified_band.png still contains PMP-WD in legend. Not referenced in paper text but exists in figures directory. Could contaminate LaTeX compilation if accidentally included.
Proposition 1 (composition closure: product of non-negative functions is non-negative) is trivially obvious and inflates theoretical contribution appearance.

VGG-16-BN tested only with SGD/CIFAR-10 (21 runs). Architecture x optimizer effects fully confounded. Paper cannot distinguish whether invariance is due to BN, optimizer, or architecture without VGG+AdamW comparison.
issues_fixed: Figure 8 (theorem2_validation.png) PMP-WD ghost data: FIXED. Figure now shows 7 methods, N=21 data points, correlations recomputed correctly (rho=-0.161/p=0.485, rho=0.107/p=0.645). (iter_010 C2: FIXED)
Figure 5 (diagnostic heatmap) half_lambda BEM=0.000: FIXED. Now shows BEM=0.500, matching Table 6. (iter_010 C1: FIXED, confirmed by supervisor cross-validation)
Title 'Why' overclaim: FIXED. Changed to 'When', removing causation claim that was unsupported by evidence. (iter_010 C8: FIXED)
Alignment analysis text updated to N=21 with correct rho/p values after PMP-WD removal. (iter_010 C6: PARTIALLY FIXED -- framing improved but section still occupies excessive space)
